nodes.context: Criminal family from Acquasanta, Palermo; controlled docks and wholesale market rackets.
Sicilian Mafia organization; overarching structure for Palermo clans.
Mafia clan from southeast Palermo; part of 'Mafia of the Gardens'.
Mafia clan from southeast Palermo; part of 'Mafia of the Gardens'.
Mafia clan from southeast Palermo; part of 'Mafia of the Gardens'.
Faction led by Michele Cavataio; took over Acquasanta after internal betrayal.
Mafia faction from Corleone led by Salvatore Riina; expanded power in Palermo.
Resuttana Mafia family; strong allies of Galatolo clan.
Mafia mandamento ruled by Rosario Riccobono; included Resuttana before split.
Mafia mandamento in Palermo; ruled by Galatolo and Madonia clans.
Mafia clan from San Lorenzo; led by Giuseppe Giacomo Gambino.
Faction of Greco clan from Croceverde Giardini; allied with Corleonesi.
Faction of Greco clan from Ciaculli; fought with Croceverde Giardini faction.

edges.evidence_quote: The Acquasanta Mafia clan tried to muscle in on the protection racket that traditionally belonged the 'Mafia of the Gardens' — such as the Greco, Bontade and Sorci clans from the southeast of Palermo
edges.context: Galatolo clan fought Greco clan over control of wholesale market racket.

edges.evidence_quote: The Acquasanta Mafia clan tried to muscle in on the protection racket that traditionally belonged the 'Mafia of the Gardens' — such as the Greco, Bontade and Sorci clans from the southeast of Palermo
edges.context: Galatolo clan fought Bontade clan over control of wholesale market racket.

edges.evidence_quote: The Acquasanta Mafia clan tried to muscle in on the protection racket that traditionally belonged the 'Mafia of the Gardens' — such as the Greco, Bontade and Sorci clans from the southeast of Palermo
edges.context: Galatolo clan fought Sorci clan over control of wholesale market racket.
Cavataio faction betrayed and killed Galatolo leaders during the wholesale market war.
Galatolo clan allied with Corleonesi during the Second Mafia War.

edges.evidence_quote: The Acquasanta family thus now answered to the Madonia clan, strong allies of the Galatolos.
edges.context: Galatolo clan was subordinate to and allied with Madonia clan.

edges.evidence_quote: Vito Galatolo became the new leader of the Resuttana mandamento
edges.context: Galatolo clan ruled the Resuttana mandamento.

edges.evidence_quote: Riina was backed by many of the allies he had spent the last decade gathering in the city, especially Francesco Madonia
edges.context: Madonia clan was a key ally of the Corleonesi.

edges.evidence_quote: Riccobono had been appointed with the strong support of Salvatore Greco and Stefano Bontade
edges.context: Greco and Bontade clans were allies supporting Riccobono.

edges.evidence_quote: Michele Greco, leader of a different faction of the Greco clan hailing from Croceverde Giardini, which had fought its own war with the Ciaculli Grecos in the 1940s.
edges.context: Two Greco factions fought each other in the 1940s.

edges.evidence_quote: Riina was backed by ... Michele Greco, leader of a different faction of the Greco clan hailing from Croceverde Giardini
edges.context: Croceverde Giardini Greco clan allied with Corleonesi.

edges.evidence_quote: their territory was initially under the jurisdiction of the Partanna-Mondello mandamento ruled by Rosario Riccobono
edges.context: Resuttana was part of Partanna-Mondello mandamento before being split off.
